name: TAO 4h Failed Breakdown Hold Continuation v1
edge_mechanism: a downside break fails, price reclaims support, then holds that reclaimed structure before continuation entry into resumed trend
thesis: The cycle concept set explicitly includes failed breakdown reclaim that must hold before continuation entry. TAO 4h is the best-fit lane because it is the only allowed 4h asset currently showing real trend-up contamination. The edge is not simple dip-buying. It is joining the resumed move only after the failed breakdown proves that reclaimed support can hold.
invalidation_condition: If TAO pullbacks are actually regime deterioration rather than failed breakdowns, the hold entry will repeatedly buy into fresh downside continuation.

pre_submit_reasoning.market_behavior_targeted: A downside break under local support fails, price reclaims support, that reclaimed support holds, and continuation resumes in the dominant upward structure.
pre_submit_reasoning.why_edge_should_exist: Failed breakdowns in trend-up regimes often trap late sellers and create strong continuation once support is reclaimed and defended.
pre_submit_reasoning.asset_timeframe_fit: TAO 4h fits best because it carries actual trend-up structure while still being in the allowed basket. BTC and ETH are more chop-dominant, so this specific continuation grammar is less natural there.
pre_submit_reasoning.likely_failure_mode: Support reclaims may occur inside broader deterioration, causing the setup to repeatedly enter failed bounces.
pre_submit_reasoning.first_refinement_path: If the reclaim trades but hold failures dominate, tighten the hold definition before touching the higher-timeframe trend anchor.
pre_submit_reasoning.expected_outcome_pattern: Fewer trades than the reversion idea, but higher asymmetry when continuation survives the hold.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_success: TAO 4h produces enough reclaimed-support continuations to clear screen density and one cross-lane shows a weaker but visible version of the grammar.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_failure: Sparse participation, repeated failed holds, or the setup working only by accident on one lane with no portability.

density_sanity_check.event_sparsity: moderate but acceptable because the reclaim-hold logic is compact and thesis-aligned

validation_basket.why_included: Best-fit structurally different lane for failed-breakdown continuation.
validation_basket.continue_if: TAO 4h shows enough reclaimed-support holds with positive continuation asymmetry.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: TAO 4h keeps treating reclaimed support as temporary bounce territory.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Moderate count and larger runner contribution than on BTC or ETH.

validation_basket.why_included: Checks whether the same reclaim-hold logic survives on the cycle primary lane even if it is not the best fit.
validation_basket.continue_if: BTC 4h preserves some reclaimed-support continuation without collapsing into chop noise.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: BTC 4h proves the mechanism is too continuation-dependent for the current regime.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Lower count, smaller but cleaner follow-through.

validation_basket.why_included: Tests if the same pattern can degrade gracefully on a more range-bound major.
validation_basket.continue_if: ETH 4h still produces a smaller but valid reclaim-hold continuation sample.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: ETH 4h is too chop-bound to express continuation after the hold.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Low-to-moderate count, lower asymmetry than TAO.

long: Enter only if next bar low stays >= EMA_20 - 0.15 * ATR_14 and close remains above EMA_20
short: Enter only if next bar high stays <= EMA_20 + 0.15 * ATR_14 and close remains below EMA_20

long: Scale in: add 25% if trade reaches +0.55 * ATR_14 and close remains above EMA_20
Take profit: 0.70 * ATR_14 on 35% of initial size
Take profit: 1.35 * ATR_14 on 30% of total size
short: Scale in: add 25% if trade reaches +0.55 * ATR_14 and close remains below EMA_20
Take profit: 0.70 * ATR_14 on 35% of initial size
Take profit: 1.35 * ATR_14 on 30% of total size
